acceptance_targets: [CH12_CRAFT_RECOGNITION_OVERCERTIFIED] After the revised trigger at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:183, a reader should be able to point to one decisive recognition sequence and one clear deferment choice. Pass only if the later material anchored near snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:241 functions as fallout from the reveal instead of a second recap that re-announces Damian's realization in new wording.

[CH12_CRAFT_KREUZFELD_OPAQUE] By the end of the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:269 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:285, a cold reader can answer three questions from the text itself: what was supposed to happen at Kreuzfeld, what failed, and why Anya cannot handle both the border problem and Damian's recognition at once. Fail if any of those answers still depends on prior-chapter memory rather than this chapter's wording.
[LW05-002] Within the first page of chapter 12, the reader knows how much time has passed since chapter 11 and what became of the 0600 Falk watch and the 'Yor one more night' calculation.
[LW05-004] When Damian recognizes Anya in chapter 12, the reader can point to a distinct new trigger or new configuration of evidence, not simply the same Eden anecdotes being restaged in public.
[LW05-005] Across chapters 10-12, only one scene uses Anya's apparent hunger or thinness as the primary proof of care; the others show concern through distinct, character-specific observations.
[LW06-003] Within the opening pages of chapter 12, the reader should feel the 11/12 boundary tightening pressure rather than suspending it.
[length_chapter_12_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: After the revised trigger at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:183, a reader should be able to point to one decisive recognition sequence and one clear deferment choice. Pass only if the later material anchored near snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:241 functions as fallout from the reveal instead of a second recap that re-announces Damian's realization in new wording.
findings.problem: Damian's recognition detonation lands, then gets certified again and again through a checklist of remembered clues and repeated thesis-thoughts. The scene keeps restating that he knows instead of trusting the first impact, so the reveal reads as processed and annotated rather than violently immediate.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the recognition sequence centered on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:185; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:191; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:201; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:211; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:217; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:241; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:249; and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:253 so it resolves once, then moves into consequence. Preserve three beats only: Erich's trigger, Damian's identification, and Damian's decision not to expose her in the room. Remove the extra layers that restate the same realization in separate narrative and italicized forms.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:269 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:285, a cold reader can answer three questions from the text itself: what was supposed to happen at Kreuzfeld, what failed, and why Anya cannot handle both the border problem and Damian's recognition at once. Fail if any of those answers still depends on prior-chapter memory rather than this chapter's wording.
findings.problem: The chapter asks the Kreuzfeld alert to operate as a co-equal pressure with Damian's recognition, but its logic stays coded and abstract on the page. The reader gets a checkpoint name, a terse pager message, and vague references to a route and 'papers' without a clean statement of what is supposed to cross, what failed, or what immediate consequence Anya is weighing.
findings.rewrite_direction:
Clarify the border-pressure thread at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:105; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:107; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:109; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:269; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:273; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:283; and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:285 with one compact orienting thought that identifies what Kreuzfeld is carrying tonight, what 'MISSED' means operationally, and why Damian's thought about moved papers matters. Keep the signal traffic terse, but make the immediate cause-and-effect legible from this chapter alone.

Aggregator guidance:
- Cross-chapter context: This border-pressure thread pays off immediately after the gala: Chapter 13 delivers Crane's death at a border checkpoint and frames it as part of the blind-channel fallout Falk exploited. Clarify Chapter 12 so Kreuzfeld reads as a specific transfer/watch failure leading into that pre-dawn checkpoint consequence, without prematurely naming Falk.

findings.acceptance_test: Within the first page of chapter 12, the reader knows how much time has passed since chapter 11 and what became of the 0600 Falk watch and the 'Yor one more night' calculation.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3110; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3116; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3125; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3139; Chapter 11 exits on a specific dawn task: Anya writes "0600 / diplomatic procurement desk / Falk" and explicitly decides Yor can wait one more night because that WISE lane is about to go blind at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3110 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3118. Chapter 12 then opens at Becky's gala with the mole clock still active at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3125 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3139; but never says what the 0600 watch showed or why it was superseded.

evidence.excerpt: 3116: Yor one more night.

3125: The Blackbell estate glowed against the October dark with the restraint of someone who didn't know the word. White lanterns lined the drive at meticulous intervals. The topiary garden had been strung with lights that made it look like a magazine spread, which it would be. Becky had confirmed photographers from three publications, all carefully vetted to make the event look effortless instead of like the three weeks of controlled panic it had actually been.

3139: Damian came around the car. Dark suit, precise posture. The thin scar through his eyebrow was the only thing that didn't match the packaging. He hadn't said much on the drive over. The mole was still unidentified. The Renewal Front's clock had shrunk to days. Attending a charity gala while all of that pressed at their backs felt obscene, but covers needed maintenance. Anya's social calendar had to look like a real person's social calendar.

3118: It was ugly and it was still the calculation. Her mother was somewhere in the city with Dominic's photographs and whatever was left of her faith in Garden. Anya was betting Yor would stay quiet until tomorrow because the other clock had reached six in the morning and a desk inside WISE was about to go blind.

findings.problem: Chapter 11 closes on a precise countdown: 0600 at the diplomatic procurement desk, with Yor explicitly granted only 'one more night.' Chapter 12 opens at Becky's gala with no immediate cue about whether the 0600 watch already happened, failed, or was overtaken. The result is a boundary where the reader has to stop and reconstruct the clock instead of being carried forward by it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Before the gala texture takes over, add one orienting sentence that states what became of the 0600 Falk watch and how that result interacts with Yor's "one more night" clock, so the reader enters Chapter 12 already oriented in time and consequence.

findings.acceptance_test: When Damian recognizes Anya in chapter 12, the reader can point to a distinct new trigger or new configuration of evidence, not simply the same Eden anecdotes being restaged in public.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2580; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2596; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2600; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3283; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3305; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3333

evidence.excerpt: 2580: "After the dodgeball tournament. Losing team had to clean the gym. Floors, chairs, all of it." One finger touched the butterfly strips as if checking whether the wound was still there. "My team lost because a girl punched me."

2596: "And then there was the school festival cleanup." His eyes stayed half-closed, voice drifting with exhaustion. "Poster paints everywhere. Blue all over the floor. There was this girl on the cleanup crew laughing so hard she kept sliding when she tried to mop." The thought underneath arrived less neatly. *Pink hair. Blue shoes. Same laugh. Same tilt of the head when--*
2600: "You ever go to Eden?" he asked.

3283: The projector clacked. The wall filled with school festival cleanup day: a row of twelve-year-olds holding brooms and dustpans with the expressions of prisoners assigned to yard work. Anya was in the frame. So was Damian. They stood shoulder to shoulder with the body language of two people who would rather have been anywhere else.

3305: "Wait." His whole face lit with the loose clarity alcohol stripped him down to. "Wait, wait. You and Anya Forger had that whole thing in school, right?" He was talking to Damian now, jabbing the beer toward the projected photo. "The rivalry or whatever. She punched you once, didn't she? During dodgeball?"
3333: *She's Anya Forger.*

findings.problem: Chapter 9 already runs Damian through the exact Eden-memory chain that matters most: the dodgeball punch, the cleanup duty, the laugh, and the direct question about Eden. Chapter 12 then needs the gala to replay the same recognition machinery before the name finally clicks. Because the later scene reuses the earlier scene's specific triggers instead of escalating with materially new evidence, Damian's 'detonation' lands as a second pass through familiar setup.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either trim chapter 9's near-recognition so it plants less fully articulated school memory, or change chapter 12's trigger package so the gala recognition locks through a genuinely new pressure point rather than replaying dodgeball plus cleanup plus pink-hair recall.

findings.acceptance_test: Across chapters 10-12, only one scene uses Anya's apparent hunger or thinness as the primary proof of care; the others show concern through distinct, character-specific observations.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2881; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2885; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3022; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3036; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3165; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3167

evidence.excerpt: 2881: Anya looked too thin. She always looked thin. Yor worried about this the way she worried about the big things, not the milk or the soup, which were small worries that had solutions, but the kind of worry that lived in her chest and didn't go away because Anya was twenty and an adult and you couldn't make an adult eat.
2885: "You should eat more," Yor said. "You look thin."
3022: "You look tired," Falk said without lifting his eyes from the digest. "Eat breakfast."
3036: *She doesn't eat enough. Loid used to do the same thing.*

3165: "You came!" She grabbed Anya by the shoulders, held her at arm's length, and ran a brisk head-to-heels check that could've meant deep affection or quality control. "You actually came. I was going to be so mad if you bailed."
3167: *She looks tired. She always looks tired now. Why doesn't she eat? I'm going to make her eat if I have to physically drag her to the buffet.*

findings.problem: Three consecutive chapters use almost the same caretaking beat to signal that someone cares about Anya: Yor thinks she looks too thin and tells her to eat more, Falk tells her to eat breakfast and thinks she doesn't eat enough, and Becky immediately clocks that she looks tired and needs feeding. In a cluster read, the repeated beat starts to sound like the default shorthand for affection rather than a relationship-specific observation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the strongest version of this beat and vary the later ones. Becky especially should notice something more specific to her history with Anya than another hunger/thinness cue, so her concern reads as Becky-shaped rather than as the third pass of the same signal.

findings.acceptance_test: Within the opening pages of chapter 12, the reader should feel the 11/12 boundary tightening pressure rather than suspending it.

3118: It was ugly and it was still the calculation. Her mother was somewhere in the city with Dominic's photographs and whatever was left of her faith in Garden. 3249: He moved away. The almost-smile stayed in her chest longer than it should have, and she hated that, because her brain should have been on the mole, on Kreuzfeld, on the clock, on anything except the exact way his mouth moved when he was trying not to be amused.

3305: "Wait." His whole face lit with the loose clarity alcohol stripped him down to. "Wait, wait. You and Anya Forger had that whole thing in school, right?" He was talking to Damian now, jabbing the beer toward the projected photo. "The rivalry or whatever. She punched you once, didn't she? During dodgeball?"

3227: Near the bar, the off-duty SSS officer from the drive accepted a refill and laughed at a joke he wasn't listening to. Under the laugh his mind ran on a separate track. *Kreuzfeld moved to before dawn. Good. Let them hit wire instead of fog for once.*
3383: Her pager vibrated inside her evening bag.

findings.problem: Because the prior chapter hands off a live operational clock, the first stretch of chapter 12 reads like a temporary pressure leak. The gala material is not useless, but the window stalls before it creates a new equivalent source of leverage or danger.
findings.rewrite_direction: Move active pressure onto the opening pages of Chapter 12: report the dawn surveillance result immediately and let Kreuzfeld or another live consequence ride under the gala from the start, rather than waiting for later pager traffic to restore momentum.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
